paper_title: The impact of personality traits on the ethical leadership, job performance and job satisfaction of public servants -A case study of the Northern public sector of Vietnam

entities.description: The field of study focusing on managing people within an organization, specifically in the public sector.
Lead author of the study from East Asia University of Technology.
Co-author from Can Tho City Institute for Socio - Economic Development Studies.
Co-author from Can Tho City Party Committee.
The academic institution where Nguyen Danh Nam is affiliated.

entities.id: Can Tho City Institute for Socio - Economic Development Studies
entities.description: The institution where Le Chi Phuong is affiliated.
The institution where Le Thi Truc Phuong is affiliated.
A taxonomy for personality traits including Openness, Conscientiousness, Extroversion, Agreeableness, and Neuroticism.
Leadership style focused on appropriate conduct through personal actions and interpersonal relationships.
The behavioral and outcome-based effectiveness of an employee in their role.
The level of content a person feels regarding their job.
Research strategy focusing on quantifying the collection and analysis of data.
An instrument used to obtain information from public servants in the study.
Statistical software used for demographic information analysis.
Software used to process Structural Equation Modeling (SEM).
A personality inventory used to measure the Big Five traits.
The final processed numeric survey data collected from public servants.
The percentage of usable survey responses returned.
